verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the β-subunit of the F1 catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase. The protein is a structural component of the inner mitochondrial membrane-facing catalytic domain, localized to the mitochondrial matrix side of Complex V. It is not a surface-exposed protein and has no documented surface-trafficking isoforms, cell-state-induced externalization, or stable surface-anchoring partners. HGNC gene-group membership (mitochondrial complex V, ATPase family) confirms mitochondrial localization. No therapeutic programs target this protein on the cell surface, and no cancer-cell ecto-display or cell-death-related externalization has been documented for this structural mitochondrial enzyme subunit.
